title: Enakku Piditha Padal Song Lyrics Julie Ganapathy
stanzas:
Enakku Piditha Padal Song Lyrics Julie Ganapathy
#Ilaiyaraja #ShreyaGhosal

[ Enaku piditha paadal
adhu unakkum pudikkume
un manadhu pogum
vazhiyai endhan manadhu
ariyume.

ennai piditha nilavu
adhu unnai pidikkume
kadhal noaikku marandhu
thandhu noaiyai koottume.

udhirvadhu pookkalaa
manadhu valartha solaiye
kadhal pookkal udhirumaa] (2)

mella nerungidum podhu
nee dhoora pogiraai
vittu vilagidum podhu
nee nerungi varugiraai

vaanathil parakkiren
mogathil midhakkiren
kadhalaal naanum orr
kaathaadi aagiren.

velli kambigalai pole
oru thooral podutho..
vinnum mannil vandhu sera
adhu paalam podutho..

neerthuli theendinaal
nee thodum niyaabagam
nee thotta idamellaam
veenaiyin thaen swaram.

aayinum aruviyaai
anbile nenaikkiraai
megam pola enakkulle
mogam valarthu kalaigiraai

Enaku piditha paadal
adhu unakkum pudikkume
un manadhu pogum
vazhiyai endhan manadhu
ariyume.

ennai piditha nilavu
adhu unnai pidikkume
kadhal noaikku marandhu
thandhu noaiyai koottume.

udhirvadhu pookkalaa
manadhu valartha solaiye
kadhal pookkal udhirumaa

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses deep feelings of love and longing, portraying the emotional connection between two lovers.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

In Julie Ganapathy, this song highlights the romantic moments between the lead characters, emphasizing their emotional bond.

Who is the singer of this song?

The song is sung by the talented Shreya Ghoshal, known for her soulful voice and emotive singing style.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Enakku Piditha Padal was composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja, renowned for his contributions to Tamil cinema music.

When was this song released?

This song was released on February 14, 2003, coinciding with Valentine's Day, making it a popular choice for romantic occasions.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Enakku Piditha Padal, the protagonist expresses a profound yearning for love, beautifully conveyed through Shreya Ghoshal's soulful rendition. The song captures the essence of romance and the bittersweet emotions that accompany it, making it a poignant moment in Julie Ganapathy. With Ilaiyaraaja's enchanting composition, the melody evokes feelings of nostalgia and longing. This heartfelt track resonates with anyone who has experienced the complexities of love.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Enakku Piditha Padal from Julie Ganapathy is a mesmerizing melody that captures the essence of love and longing. With Shreya Ghoshal's enchanting vocals paired with Ilaiyaraaja's masterful composition, this song takes listeners on an emotional journey. Experience the heartfelt lyrics that resonate deeply, making it a perfect addition to any romantic playlist.
stanzaMeanings: The song that I love, will you also like it? My heart knows the way to your heart.
The moon that loves me, will also love you; it forgets the pain of love and brings together the suffering.
The blooming flowers of love will grow in the garden of my heart.
When you slowly move away, you go far; when you leave, you come back closer.
The festival of love dances in the eyes, like a child, my heart is lost.
I soar in the sky, I float in the air; because of love, I have become a puppet.
Like silver threads, will a drizzle fall? Will it come down from the sky and meet the earth?
When the raindrop touches you, it becomes a memory; every place you touch becomes the sweet sound of the veena.
You think of me like a waterfall; like a cloud, you grow love inside me.
The song that I love, will you also like it? My heart knows the way to your heart.
The moon that loves me, will also love you; it forgets the pain of love and brings together the suffering.
The blooming flowers of love will grow in the garden of my heart.
